title: Haloragis nodulosa
taxonomy: Haloragis nodulosa is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Haloragaceae. It is native to Australia and is also known as the button bush or button wattle. This plant is further classified as belonging to the genus Haloragis, which consists of several other similar species. The Haloragis nodulosa is a perennial herb that grows in wetlands and waterways, and it is often found in areas with shallow water. It is a member of the order Haloragales, which is a small group of aquatic plants that are closely related to the water milfoils and the spiny rushes.

intro: Haloragis nodulosa, also known as spike watermilfoil, is a perennial aquatic plant native to Australia and Tasmania. This plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can be used to treat various ailments such as wounds, fever, and digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of Haloragis nodulosa include alkaloids, glycosides, and phenolic compounds, which contribute to its medicinal properties.
